UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile metadata stored in Firestore, complementing Firebase Authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication user ID.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Device.title: Device
Device.description: Represents an ESP32 camera device registered by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Device entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this device belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Device)

name.description: User-assigned friendly name for the device (e.g., 'Living Room SkyCam').

model.description: The specific model or type of the ESP32 camera.

firmwareVersion.description: The current firmware version running on the device.

lastSeen.description: Timestamp of the last successful communication from the device to the SkySnap backend.

connectionMode.description: The current or preferred connection mode of the device ('wifi' or 'bluetooth').

wifiSSID.description: The SSID of the WiFi network the device is configured to connect to.

wifiStatus.description: The last known status of the device's WiFi connection ('connected', 'failed', 'unknown').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the device was registered in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the device's information was last updated.

Capture.title: Capture
Capture.description: Represents a single multi-frame image capture session and its processing state.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Capture entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that initiated this capture. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Capture)

deviceId.description: Reference to the Device that performed this capture. (Relationship: Device 1:N Capture)

timestamp.description: The exact date and time the multi-frame capture sequence began on the device.

status.description: The current processing status of the capture ('pending', 'processing', 'done', 'error').

frameCount.description: The total number of individual raw JPEG frames captured in this burst.

exposures.description: An array of numeric exposure values (e.g., shutter speed, ISO) for each raw frame in the burst.

processedImageUrl.description: The publicly accessible URL to the final, high-dynamic-range, and processed image stored in Firebase Storage.

rawImagesStoragePath.description: The base path within Firebase Storage where all raw burst frames for this capture are stored (e.g., '/raw/{uid}/{captureId}/').

processingErrorDetails.description: Detailed message or code describing any errors encountered during the image processing pipeline.

processingDurationSeconds.description: The total time, in seconds, taken by the cloud function to process this capture.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the capture record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the capture record was last updated.

DeviceSettings.title: DeviceSettings
DeviceSettings.description: Stores user-configurable settings specific to a registered ESP32 device.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DeviceSettings entity. This could optionally be identical to the deviceId if it's a 1:1 relationship.

deviceId.description: Reference to the Device these settings apply to. (Relationship: Device 1:1 DeviceSettings)

processingMode.description: Determines where image processing occurs ('cloud' or 'local' on phone).

captureIntervalSeconds.description: The automated interval, in seconds, between consecutive image captures if scheduled.

uploadQuality.description: The JPEG compression quality (0-100) for raw frames uploaded from the device.

enableAutoWallpaper.description: Flag indicating if the app should automatically update the device wallpaper with new processed images (Android only).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the device settings record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the device settings record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching `userId` in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores information about an ESP32 camera device owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized `userId` field for robust authorization and efficient queries, aligning with the `userId` in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this device, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: deviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the registered ESP32 camera device.

definition.description: Stores user-configurable settings specific to a registered ESP32 device. This path includes `userId` for authorization checks. The `deviceId` field is denormalized within the document for clarity, and `settingsId` is expected to be a unique identifier, often matching `deviceId` for 1:1 relationships.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent device.

params.name: deviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent device to which these settings apply.

params.name: settingsId
params.description: The unique identifier for the device settings document (e.g., matching the `deviceId` if it's a 1:1 relationship).

definition.description: Records details of a multi-frame image capture session. Includes denormalized `userId` and `deviceId` fields within the document for authorization independence, efficient queries (QAPs), and data integrity, aligning with the `userId` and `deviceId` in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated this capture, corresponding to the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: deviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the device that performed this capture.

params.name: captureId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific image capture session.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. All user-owned data is organized under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, followed by nested subcollections for `devices`, `deviceSettings`, and `captures`. This hierarchical path-based ownership is the primary mechanism for authorization.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This structure ensures authorization independence by making the `userId` available as a wildcard in every relevant path (`/users/{userId}/...`). This allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid == userId` directly, eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents to fetch ownership information. Furthermore, `Device` and `Capture` documents explicitly denormalize `userId` (and `deviceId` for `Capture`) within their own fields. This denormalization supports robust querying (QAPs) and reinforces ownership, making the security posture explicit and easy to audit without relying on `get()` operations. For `DeviceSettings`, while `userId` is available in the path, `deviceId` is denormalized within the document itself, further ensuring that authorization logic for settings can be self-contained.

**QAP (Query as a Principle) Support:** The structured segregation of data enables efficient and secure `list` operations (QAPs). For example:
*   To list a user's devices: A query on `/users/{userId}/devices` can be secured by `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`.
*   To list a device's captures: A query on `/users/{userId}/devices/{deviceId}/captures` can be secured by the same rule, as `userId` is part of the path.
The explicit `userId` and `deviceId` fields within `Device` and `Capture` documents also allow for more flexible index-based queries should the application need to, for instance, query all captures by a specific device across users (though not part of current requirements, it provides scalability).

**Structural Segregation and Predictability:** Each collection (e.g., `devices`, `captures`, `deviceSettings`) holds documents of a homogeneous security posture (all owned by the `userId` in the path). This simplifies security rules greatly as there are no mixed access requirements within a single collection. The use of descriptive wildcards (`userId`, `deviceId`, `captureId`, `settingsId`) and consistent field naming (e.g., `userId` as the owner reference) enhances clarity and debuggability.
